title: Central Bank of Iran -- Official/NIMA-Weighted Exchange Rates Page (Daily Snapshot)
source_org: Central Bank of the Islamic Republic of Iran (cbi.ir)
time_coverage: 2026-07-14 (1405/04/23), single-day snapshot; page is a live/current-day rates table, not a historical archive
frequency: daily (page updates daily; this is one day's snapshot)
units: Rial per foreign-currency unit (Iran's own official currency, no toman conversion needed)
license_terms: Public government data, no API key required, not geo-blocked from this agent's fetch (contrary to the brief's expectation) -- fetched directly via both WebFetch and curl with no errors.
notes: USD/IRR = 1,359,712 rial as of 1405/04/23, described on the page as the NIMA/weighted rate ('نرخ موزون بر مبنای حجم معاملات در سامانه معاملات ارز' -- weighted rate based on transaction volume in the electronic FX trading system), i.e. this is CBI's own current OFFICIAL-side reference rate, not the parallel/black-market rate. Compared to the existing fx__official_vs_parallel_gap_irn chart's OFFICIAL variant, whose last point is 42,000 rial/USD for 2019-2023 (WDI PA.NUS.FCRF, frozen at the old subsidized/preferential rate and clearly stale -- WDI has not tracked Iran's official rate's actual multi-tier drift since roughly 2018). CBI's own live rate shows the official/NIMA rate has itself moved to ~1.36M rial/USD, a dramatic and informative update to the OFFICIAL series that WDI's stale figure completely misses. This is a single fresh anchor point, not a historical series -- did not attempt to reconstruct 2024-2026 official-rate history from Wayback Machine snapshots of this same page (out of scope per brief's 'secondary priority, do not spend long here'; flagged as a good target for a future dedicated CBI-history pull).
